title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission · Language Clinic: Discovery Manifest
summary: Recursive discovery follows a symlink outside content, includes hidden drafts and generated output, treats directories as documents, and overwrites two sources onto one route. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the discovery manifest increment in bounded content-discovery manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Record stable source identity, relative path, size, digest purpose, route candidate, status, and rejection reason.
Detect case, Unicode, normalization, index, extension, and slug collisions before any output write.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Discovery Manifest · Theory Discovery Manifest
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 5756 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-paths-content-discovery-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-paths-content-discovery-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_paths_content_discovery_3(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Discovery Manifest evidence · Theory Discovery Manifest
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-discovery-manifest-18sdkj
# Competency: ssg-discovery-manifest.
# Case 5734: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_discovery_manifest_18sdkj(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_18sdkj = "18sdkj-jkds81-5734"
    assert evidence_variant_18sdkj
    evidence_axes_18sdkj = (
        "reader18sdkj",
        "source5734",
        "parsejkds81",
        "route18sdkj5734",
        "artifact573418sdkj",
        "recoveryjkds815734",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_18sdkj)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"

Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable source identity, relative path, size, digest purpose, route candidate, status, and rejection reason.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Discovery Manifest model · Theory Discovery Manifest.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record stable source identity, relative path, size, digest purpose, route candidate, status, and rejection reason.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Discovery Manifest model · Theory Discovery Manifest, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Discovery Manifest precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Discovery Manifest model · Theory Discovery Manifest, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5374 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable source identity, relative path, size, digest purpose, route candidate, status, and rejection reason.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Discovery Manifest model · Theory Discovery Manifest, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: The list of rendered pages is enough to reproduce discovery.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-discovery-manifest-18sdkj(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_discovery_manifest_18sdkj\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(discovered, admitted, rejected\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted \+ rejected != discovered)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?root_escapes > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?route_collisions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
